evidence: Lightbox render-model assembly remains duplicated across image/video paths: `useImageLightboxRenderModel` in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx` (e.g. lightboxState/layout/workflow assembly around lines 550-852) and `useVideoLightboxRenderModel` in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` (parallel assembly around lines 176-530).

Inline edit still creates a separate integration seam by directly composing MediaLightbox internals (`useInlineEditState` imports/assembles many hooks from `@/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks` and contexts) instead of going through the main lightbox controller path.

Task creation boundaries still leak weakly typed params: `createIndividualTravelSegmentTask` reads `parsed_resolution_wh` through `(params as unknown as Record<string, unknown>)` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts:714`.

Payload read precedence is inconsistent across consumers: `ImageGenerationDetails` manually prefers different fields/order than `VideoTravelDetails` (which uses `buildTaskPayloadSnapshot` + `createTravelPayloadReader` precedence helpers), creating cross-surface drift risk.

Previously flagged issues in this dimension appear fixed: `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` now converts failed `OperationResult` values via `toOperationResultError` and throws, `calculate-task-cost` maps `SubTaskLookupError.stage` to stage-specific codes, and `rateLimit` now carries degraded metadata.

`src/shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts` still throws generic `Error` wrappers (`Failed to load ... settings`, `Network connection issue...`) and classifies failures via `errorMsg.includes(...)`, which drops structured error identity and cause chains.

`src/tools/join-clips/lib/clipInitService.ts` uses silent `catch {}` fallbacks for localStorage paths and does not validate `parseInt` output; malformed persisted values can propagate as `NaN` into UI skeleton sizing in `src/tools/join-clips/pages/JoinClipsPage.tsx`.
error_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Error propagation is still split between structured `OperationResult` contracts and ad-hoc string/generic-error fallbacks, causing context loss and less predictable caller behavior.

findings.summary: Image and video lightbox flows still maintain duplicated render-model assembly contracts, so behavior changes must be manually synchronized across forks.
evidence: `ImageLightbox` builds `lightboxVariants`, `lightboxStateValue`, `workflowBar`, `workflowControls`, and `layoutProps` inline in one hook (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:550-852`).

`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` re-implements the same state/layout/workflow assembly shape with video-specific deltas (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:176-530`).
Both paths then feed the same `LightboxLayout` and `LightboxShell`, so the seam is duplicated in adapters instead of centralized.
findings.suggestion: Extract one shared render-model composer with capability adapters (image/video feature providers) so shared state/layout/workflow wiring is single-source, and modality-specific logic is isolated to small strategy functions.

findings.summary: Inline edit still assembles MediaLightbox editing primitives through a separate path, creating a third controller seam and increasing drift risk.
evidence: `useInlineEditState` imports and wires `useInpainting`, `useMagicEditMode`, `useRepositionMode`, `useImg2ImgMode`, `useEditSettingsPersistence`, and related MediaLightbox internals directly (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:17-34`, `124-220`, `319+`).

`InlineEditView` recreates canvas/control/sidebar composition with `MediaDisplayWithCanvas`, `EditModePanel`, button groups, and providers (`src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx:109-189`, `217-275`).

Main lightbox flow already has an independent composition pipeline (`useImageLightboxSharedState`/`useImageLightboxEditing`/`useImageLightboxRenderModel` in `ImageLightbox.tsx`), so edit behavior now exists in parallel orchestration surfaces.
findings.suggestion: Route inline edit through a shared lightbox edit-controller contract (or extract a reusable edit orchestration module consumed by both lightbox and inline edit) so edit lifecycle, settings sync, and action wiring are authored once.

findings.summary: Individual travel-segment task creation still bypasses typed input contracts via double-cast access for resolution, keeping a weak seam at task boundary.
evidence: Resolution is read with `const directResolution = asString((params as unknown as Record<string, unknown>).parsed_resolution_wh);` (`src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts:714`).

The helper then merges multiple fallback sources (`originalParams.parsed_resolution_wh` and `originalParams.orchestrator_details.parsed_resolution_wh`) before calling `resolveProjectResolution` (`lines 715-724`), but the first hop is not compile-time checked.

Current tests validate many payload fields but do not assert this contract seam against malformed/non-string `parsed_resolution_wh` inputs (`src/shared/lib/tasks/__tests__/individualTravelSegment.test.ts`).
findings.suggestion: Promote `parsed_resolution_wh` into the typed task input seam (or parse params through a dedicated runtime parser before use), then remove the double cast and add regression tests for invalid resolution-shape inputs.

findings.summary: Different payload readers still apply divergent field-precedence rules, causing inconsistent cross-layer behavior for equivalent task metadata.
evidence: `ImageGenerationDetails` manually mixes top-level/original/orchestrator fields with bespoke precedence (`prompt`, `negativePrompt`, `model`, `resolution`) (`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationDetails.tsx:94-102`).

`VideoTravelDetails` uses `buildTaskPayloadSnapshot` + `createTravelPayloadReader` and explicit source-order picks (`taskViewContract`/`individualSegmentParams`/`orchestratorDetails`/`rawParams`) (`src/shared/components/TaskDetails/VideoTravelDetails.tsx:64-72`, `133-142`, `205-217`).
These surfaces are both rendering task details, but one uses ad-hoc field fallback logic while the other uses centralized reader precedence, so metadata display can disagree by route/component.
findings.suggestion: Standardize task metadata reads on shared payload-reader contracts for all details surfaces (including image details), and codify canonical precedence once in common utilities with cross-component tests.

findings.summary: Task families still hand-roll large payload translation blocks, indicating a missing shared boundary abstraction and ongoing glue-code entropy.
evidence: `imageGeneration` manually assembles `taskParamsToSend` with custom Object.assign + mapped fields (`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:417-443`).
`joinClips` builds a large `orchestratorDetails` object with per-field inclusion logic and separate per-join override loops (`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:333-439`).
`travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder` contains another monolithic orchestrator payload writer with many conditional field rules (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:114-213`).

`magicEdit` and `individualTravelSegment` perform separate bespoke payload builds and resolution/lineage mapping (`src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts:134-183`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` payload builders).
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared task-boundary composition layer (declarative field maps + reusable precedence/lineage/resolution plugins) and migrate each family to thin adapters, reducing duplicated mapper logic and cross-family divergence.

findings.summary: Travel task creation still bypasses compile-time contract with a double cast at the integration seam.
evidence: `buildTravelRequestBodyV2` returns `Record<string, unknown>` rather than the canonical task-input type.

`generateVideo` mutates this loose object (`requestBody.resolution`, `requestBody.structure_guidance`, optional `loras`) and calls `createTravelBetweenImagesTask(requestBody as unknown as TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput)`.
This preserves the previously flagged seam weakness: compile-time guarantees are bypassed at the task creation boundary.
findings.suggestion: Make `buildTravelRequestBodyV2` (and wrapper `buildTravelRequestBody`) return `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput` directly, model optional post-build fields in that type, and remove the `as unknown as` cast from `generateVideo`.

findings.summary: Travel payload readers use divergent source-precedence rules, causing non-obvious cross-layer behavior.
evidence: `travelPayloadReader` defines a default ordered source precedence via `DEFAULT_SOURCE_ORDER` and uses a family `read_contract` fallback.
`resolveTravelPairShotGenerationId` in the same module hard-codes a different order (`individualSegmentParams`, `rawParams`, `orchestratorDetails`) and ignores task-view/family contract sources.

Adjacent consumers still perform bespoke extraction paths (e.g., `generationTransformers` probing `originalParams.orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, etc.), reinforcing inconsistent boundary precedence.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical precedence contract for travel payload reads (including pair-shot ID lookup), expose it via shared reader helpers, and migrate ad-hoc extractors to that contract to eliminate drift.

findings.summary: Edge-layer payload normalization is still reimplemented per module instead of flowing through one boundary utility.
evidence: `payloadNormalization.ts` provides shared helpers (`isObjectRecord`, `asObjectRecord`, `asObjectOrEmpty`).
`billing.ts` defines separate `isObjectRecord` and `parseJsonRecord` for response parsing.
`edgeRequest.ts` defines separate `isJsonObject`/`coerceLooseObject` for request parsing.
This duplicated boundary glue increases integration entropy and makes malformed-input behavior harder to keep aligned.
findings.suggestion: Unify edge JSON/object normalization behind shared helpers (or a small shared parser module with strict/loose modes) and replace local copies in billing and edgeRequest.

findings.summary: Task status counts silently degrade to zeros on query failures, masking operational errors as valid data.
evidence: `useTaskStatusCounts` handles `Promise.allSettled` failures by logging via `handleError` but still returns `{ processing: 0, recentSuccesses: 0, recentFailures: 0 }` components for failed queries.
No degraded/error field is returned to consumers, so UI cannot distinguish failures from real zero counts.
`generationTransformers.calculateDerivedCountsSafe` already uses explicit degraded signaling (`degraded`, `errorCode`), showing an inconsistency in error strategy at similar read boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Return explicit degraded metadata from `useTaskStatusCounts` (e.g., `degraded: true`, `failedQueries`, `errorCode`) and let callers decide whether to render stale values, fallback UI, or retry indicators.

findings.summary: Tool settings fetch path converts upstream failures into generic Errors and message-string branching, losing machine-readable error context.
evidence: `extractAndMergeSettings` throws new `Error(...)` strings for scope query failures (`toolSettingsService.ts:160-168`) rather than preserving typed/coded failure metadata.
`fetchToolSettingsSupabase` then branches on `errorMsg.includes('Failed to fetch')` and `includes('Auth timeout')` (`toolSettingsService.ts:258-265`), making behavior dependent on message text.

Elsewhere in the same batch, failures are handled with explicit structured metadata (`OperationResult.errorCode/policy/recoverable` in `operationResult.ts` and `useDiscordSignIn.ts:17-41`), so this path is inconsistent with prevailing contract style.
findings.suggestion: Return a structured failure type from tool settings fetch (e.g., `OperationResult` or a typed error class with `errorCode`, `recoverable`, and `cause`) and replace message-substring checks with explicit code-based branching.

findings.summary: Join Clips storage helpers silently swallow localStorage failures and malformed cache values, creating unobservable degraded behavior.
evidence: `getCachedClipsCount` catches all errors and returns `0` (`clipInitService.ts:36-41`), while `setCachedClipsCount` also uses silent `catch {}` (`clipInitService.ts:46-54`).
`getCachedClipsCount` uses `parseInt` without `Number.isFinite` validation (`clipInitService.ts:38`), so corrupted storage can yield `NaN` while still typed as `number`.
That value feeds skeleton length math in `JoinClipsPage` (`Math.max(2, cachedClipsCount) + 1`), so malformed persistence can alter rendering without any machine-readable degraded signal.
In contrast, similar localStorage-risk code in `useDiscordSignIn` returns structured `operationFailure` with `errorCode` (`useDiscordSignIn.ts:17-24`) and logs metadata.
findings.suggestion: Normalize this path to explicit degraded signaling: validate parsed counts (`Number.isFinite` + sane bounds), emit a structured recoverable failure/log code on storage errors, and fall back to a deterministic safe count.
